author.name: Artemii Pogodin
author.headline: Motion Designer & AI Workflow Specialist | 3D & 2D | After Effects, Blender, C4D | UK Global Talent Visa

posts.content:
The Battle for the Best AI Render Engine & Breakdown:
Why personal workflows beat cloud giants 🤖🥊

When it comes to AI video generation, the biggest tradeoff is usually control versus convenience. In this breakdown of my experimental project, I put a custom ComfyUI pipeline head-to-head with Seedance 2.0, Kling AI, Weavy, and Higgsfield AI to test how different models handle complex 3D abstractions.

The main issue with cloud-based "black boxes" is that they are primarily optimized for human characters and cinematic shots. When you give them abstract geometry like this glass aquarium cube, they often struggle with the physics. This can lead to flickering, artifacts, and a loss of volume during 360-degree rotations.

🏆 The clear winner for this specific task is a custom WAN 2.1🥇 workflow. Using ControlNet with Depth and Canny maps creates a reliable bridge from raw 3D exports in Cinema 4d, Blender, or After Effects to a photorealistic render. It allows for a level of precision that is currently hard to achieve with one-click generations.

In this breakdown, you can see how a personalized pipeline preserves complex details like the water sloshing inside the ice head due to centrifugal force. Credits to Mick Mahler for the workflow and VACE logic and that makes this level of control possible.
Check out the side-by-side comparison in the video. Are you leaning more toward cloud solutions right now, or building out local workflows? Let's discuss below! 👇

#MotionDesign #AI #ComfyUI #3DRender #VFX #Breakdown #MotionGraphics #KlingAI #Seedance #Weavy #Higgsfield #AfterEffects #Houdini #Blender3D

ai.title: ComfyUI pipeline vs cloud AI render engines: handling complex 3D abstractions
ai.summary: Compared custom ComfyUI pipeline against multiple cloud AI video generators for rendering abstract 3D geometry. Found cloud services optimized for human characters/cinematic shots struggle with complex abstract forms like glass aquarium cubes.
ai.why_important: Reveals critical limitations of black-box cloud AI tools when working with non-standard 3D content, validating need for custom local pipelines.
ai.learnings: Custom ComfyUI workflows provide better control for abstract 3D renders than cloud-based AI video generators optimized for human-centric content.

author.name: Yossi Refaeli
author.headline: Engineering Mindset & Visual ai Design @ Reality Labs

posts.content:
I’ve been experimenting with a local pipeline that feels a bit like cheating.

Inspired by the workflow shared by Mickmumpitz, combine raw Blender renders and cinematography using ComfyUI and the SkyReels V3 + Wan VACE model merge.

The traditional 3D pipeline is great, but sometimes you just want to play Digital Puppet Theater and let the AI be your VFX department.

The Workflow 
The Skeleton (Blender) I block out the scene, camera moves, and basic geometry. No textures, no fancy lighting just the raw clay. 

I run a Look Dev pass on a single frame to dial in the vibe before committing to a full render.

The AI takes my 3D blueprint and fills in the rest generating realistic simulation and textures that stay pinned to my geometry.

Why this is a game changer
Zero UV Unwrapping, Dynamic FX for Free, Instant Style Swaps

ai.title: Blender + ComfyUI + SkyReels V3 + Wan VACE: raw renders to cinematic shots
ai.summary: Workflow combining raw Blender renders with ComfyUI using SkyReels V3 + Wan VACE model merge for cinematography. Blocks out scenes in Blender with basic geometry, no textures, then runs Look Dev pass on single frame before full render to dial in aesthetic.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates hybrid pipeline where AI handles VFX and cinematography while artist controls composition and camera work through traditional 3D tools.
ai.learnings: Raw Blender geometry can be used as compositional skeleton, letting merged AI models handle lighting, texturing and cinematic effects through ComfyUI.

author.name: David Roberts
author.headline: Building AI Ads and Agents (Let’s connect)

posts.content:
I built an AI system that turns listing photos into cinematic property videos. Just paste a Zillow link.

Generated this one for an apartment complex for $12 in credits. 10 minutes. No videographer. No editor.

The average property manager pays $200–$600 for professional video production.

Here's how it works inside Calico AI:

→ Pick your video length (20s, 40s, or 60s) and upload listing photos
→ Paste the listing URL — Calico scrapes the property details, neighborhood, and listing data
→ AI writes an optimized voiceover script based on the listing highlights
→ Generate a custom background music track from a text description
→ Each photo gets animated into a cinematic walkthrough-style clip
→ Calico stitches everything together with auto-generated captions

The output looks like a $1,000 production.

Property managers are posting these to socials, embedding them on listings, and sending them to prospective renters.

No film crew.
No editing software.
No waiting days for delivery.

Comment "LISTING" and I'll send you the full tutorial video & prompts (must be connected so I can DM you).

ai.title: AI system converts Zillow listings to cinematic property videos for $12 in 10 minutes
ai.summary: Built an AI system that scrapes Zillow listings, generates optimized voiceover scripts from property data, and produces cinematic property videos in 10 minutes for $12 vs traditional $200-600 production costs. System automates the entire pipeline from URL input to final video with custom background music.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates complete workflow automation for real estate video production with concrete cost and time savings compared to traditional methods.
ai.learnings: Property listing data can be automatically converted to video content by combining web scraping, AI script generation, and automated video assembly at 5-10% of traditional production costs.

posts.content:
Today we're releasing Graphic-Design-Bench (GDB) - the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating AI on professional graphic design tasks.

49 tasks. 
5 categories. 
7 frontier models. 

The 5 domains we test: 
🖼️ Layout: spatial reasoning, component detection, layer ordering 
✍️ Typography: font, size, weight, color, spacing 
📊 Infographics: SVG understanding, editing, vector generation
 🎨 Template Semantics: intent classification, template variants 
🎬 Animation: keyframes, motion types, trajectory generation

We tested against state of the art closed source models from Google, OpenAI and Anthropic.

→ Only 2 of 49 tasks are solved 
→ 22 tasks remain completely unsolved 
and the ones that aren't? Still too imprecise to be useful in any real design workflow. 
Paper and Github link in comments.

ai.title: Graphic-Design-Bench (GDB): First comprehensive benchmark for AI on professional graphic design
ai.workflow_stage: concept / ideation | layout / composition | animation / motion
ai.summary: Released Graphic-Design-Bench with 49 tasks across 5 categories testing frontier AI models on professional design work: layout spatial reasoning, typography parameters, SVG editing, template semantics, and animation trajectory generation. Evaluated 7 state-of-the-art models from Google, OpenAI and Anthropic.
ai.why_important: Provides quantifiable framework for evaluating AI performance on concrete design tasks rather than subjective assessment.
ai.learnings: Professional graphic design can be broken into measurable tasks across layout, typography, vector graphics, templates and animation that AI models can be systematically benchmarked against.

posts.content: comfyui-lumi-batcher: ComfyUI Lumi Batcher is a batch processing extension plugin designed for ComfyUI, aiming to improve workflow debugging efficiency. Traditional debugging methods require adjusting parameters one by one, while this tool significantly enhances work efficiency through batch processing capabilities.

ai.title: ComfyUI Lumi Batcher: Batch processing extension for efficient workflow debugging
ai.summary: ByteDance's ComfyUI extension that enables batch parameter testing instead of one-by-one adjustments. Significantly reduces debugging time by running multiple parameter configurations simultaneously.
ai.why_important: Eliminates the tedious manual process of testing different settings sequentially, allowing artists to compare outputs from multiple parameter sets in one pass.
ai.learnings: Batch parameter testing through dedicated tooling can dramatically speed up ComfyUI workflow optimization compared to traditional iterative debugging approaches.

author.name: Aaron Randall
author.headline: Visual AI Artist & AI Filmmaker | 20+ Years in Concept Art, Illustration & Design | Educator

posts.content:
I never thought I'd say this but Claude might be the best fight choreographer I've ever worked with in the Ai space.

I've been testing a custom system prompt I built specifically for Seedance 2.0 and the results are kind of ridiculous. The fight scene I created has weight to it. The movements connect. The choreography actually flows like someone thought about it instead of the usual AI flailing we've all seen.

In my experience Claude writes better prompts for AI video than anything else out there. There's something about how it structures motion and timing that just clicks. Pair that with Seedance 2.0 and you've got a dynamic duo that punches way above what you'd expect from AI generated video right now.

Directing and collaborating with Claude has been one of the more efficient and enjoyable parts of my workflow. It's not perfect but when you know how to art direct the process the results speak for themselves.

I broke down the whole process on The Sketch Lab. Link to the episode in the comments below.

#AIVideo #AIFilmmaking #Claude #Seedance #PromptEngineering #CreativeAI #GenerativeAI #TheSketchLab

ai.title: Custom Claude system prompt for Seedance 2.0 generates choreographed fight scenes with flow
ai.summary: Developed a custom system prompt specifically engineered for Seedance 2.0 that produces fight choreography with weight and flow. Claude's prompt structure for motion and timing outperforms other AI tools for video generation, creating connected movements instead of typical AI flailing.
ai.why_important: Shows how prompt engineering for specific AI video tools can dramatically improve motion quality and choreography coherence in action scenes.
ai.learnings: Claude's approach to structuring motion and timing in prompts produces superior results for AI video generation when paired with specialized tools like Seedance 2.0.

posts.content: ComfyUI-to-Python-Extension: A powerful tool that translates ComfyUI workflows into executable Python code.

ai.title: ComfyUI-to-Python-Extension: Translates ComfyUI workflows into executable Python
ai.summary: A tool that converts visual ComfyUI node-based workflows into standalone Python code. Enables developers to export and execute ComfyUI workflows programmatically outside the visual interface.
ai.why_important: Allows technical artists to version control, automate, and integrate ComfyUI workflows into production pipelines without manual node recreation.
ai.learnings: Converting visual workflows to code enables batch processing, CI/CD integration, and programmatic parameter control for production-scale image generation.

posts.content: ComfyUI-qwenmultiangle: A ComfyUI custom node for 3D camera angle control. Provides an interactive Three.js viewport to adjust camera angles and outputs formatted prompt strings for multi-angle image generation.

ai.title: ComfyUI-qwenmultiangle: Interactive 3D camera control for multi-angle generation
ai.summary: Custom ComfyUI node providing an interactive Three.js viewport for visual camera angle adjustment. Outputs formatted prompt strings that drive multi-angle image generation for 3D workflows.
ai.why_important: Bridges visual 3D camera manipulation with text-to-image generation, eliminating the need to manually write complex camera position prompts.
ai.learnings: Interactive 3D viewports can generate precise camera angle prompts for AI image generation, making multi-view character/object creation more intuitive than text-based approaches.

posts.content: ComfyUI-MultiGPU: This custom_node for ComfyUI adds one-click "Virtual VRAM" for any UNet and CLIP loader as well MultiGPU integration in WanVideoWrapper, managing the offload/Block Swap of layers to DRAM *or* VRAM to maximize the latent space of your card. Also includes nodes for directly loading entire components (UNet, CLIP, VAE) onto the device you choose

ai.title: ComfyUI-MultiGPU: Virtual VRAM and multi-GPU layer management for ComfyUI
ai.summary: Custom nodes enabling virtual VRAM expansion through layer offloading to DRAM or secondary GPUs. Includes one-click integration for UNet/CLIP loaders and manual device assignment for model components.
ai.why_important: Solves VRAM limitations that prevent running large models, enabling complex workflows on consumer hardware through intelligent memory management.
ai.learnings: Layer-level offloading and block swap strategies can maximize usable VRAM by strategically moving model components between GPU memory, system RAM, and multiple GPUs during inference.

posts.content: Pallaidium: PALLAIDIUM — a generative AI movie studio, seamlessly integrated into the Blender Video Editor (VSE), enabling end-to-end production from script to screen and back.

ai.title: Pallaidium: generative AI movie studio integrated into Blender VSE for script-to-screen production
ai.summary: Open-source tool that integrates generative AI capabilities directly into Blender's Video Sequence Editor. Enables complete film production workflow from script generation through final editing within a single environment.
ai.why_important: Eliminates context switching between multiple tools by bringing AI generation directly into the editing timeline.
ai.learnings: AI-powered video production can be fully integrated into existing 3D/video editing environments rather than relying on separate cloud services.

posts.content: StableGen: Transform your 3D texturing workflow with the power of generative AI, directly within Blender!

ai.title: StableGen: Generative AI texturing workflow directly in Blender
ai.summary: Blender addon that brings generative AI texturing capabilities into the 3D workflow. Allows artists to generate and apply textures without leaving their primary 3D environment.
ai.why_important: Removes friction from 3D texturing by eliminating the need to export/import between external AI tools and 3D software.
ai.learnings: Generative AI texture generation can be integrated directly into Blender's native material workflow for seamless iteration.

author.name: Scott Fegette
author.headline: I help tech companies build content, product and learning strategies for creative audiences. 30 years inside Macromedia, Adobe, Lynda.com, and LinkedIn.

posts.content:
How do you REALLY put AI models to work for you creatively? Although it can burn tokens like a campfire, using MCP apps in your workflow puts the real power of AI into the tools you use the most.  In this case, rebuilding a set of CMS views I'd built out in code quickly, so weren't reflected in the Figma design file.   What to do?  MCP to the rescue!  

Claude + GitHub + Figma x MCP = complete harmony between my design surface and my codebase... even when I'm working faster than my better judgement.
#mcp #claudecode #figma

ai.title: Claude + GitHub + Figma x MCP = complete harmony between my design surface and my codebase
ai.summary: Used Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect Claude, GitHub, and Figma for rebuilding CMS views that were coded but not reflected in design files. MCP enabled synchronization between design surface and codebase when working at high speed.
ai.why_important: Shows practical implementation of MCP for keeping design and code in sync during rapid iteration cycles.
ai.learnings: MCP apps can bridge the gap between Figma designs and code repositories to maintain consistency when development moves faster than design documentation.

ai.title: SuperSplat: 3D Gaussian Splat Editor
ai.summary: PlayCanvas' dedicated editor for 3D Gaussian Splat scenes. Provides interactive tools for inspecting, editing, and refining 3DGS captures beyond initial reconstruction.
ai.why_important: Addresses the gap between capturing 3DGS scenes and making them production-ready by offering dedicated editing capabilities for this emerging format.
ai.learnings: Specialized editors for 3D Gaussian Splatting enable post-capture refinement, suggesting the format is moving beyond research into practical creative workflows requiring editorial control.

posts.content: ComfyUI-HY-Motion1: A ComfyUI plugin based on HY-Motion 1.0 for text-to-3D human motion generation.

ai.title: ComfyUI-HY-Motion1: Text-to-3D human motion generation in ComfyUI
ai.summary: ComfyUI plugin implementing HY-Motion 1.0 model for generating 3D human motion from text descriptions. Integrates motion generation into existing ComfyUI node-based workflows.
ai.why_important: Enables text-driven character animation within ComfyUI's visual workflow system that many AI artists already use.
ai.learnings: Text-to-3D motion can be incorporated into ComfyUI pipelines alongside image generation for unified creative workflows.
